---
title: "Data_treatment"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. 

```{r}
metagraph_results <- read.delim(
   "/Users/yahya/Desktop/T-ALL_align_query.tsv",
    header = FALSE,
    sep = "\t",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
View(metagraph_results) #Reads the tsv output
```

```{r}
results_full <- data.frame() #Create an empty data frame of full results, to reformat the output
results_cutoff <- data.frame() #Create an empty data frame, for results that made the cutoff
k_cutoff = 10 #To define by the user, k-mer count cutoff to consider the k-mer as passing
coverage_cutoff = 0.8 #To define by the user, coverage cutoff to be added to the results_cutoff data frame 
lower_cutoff = 10 #Number of acceptable k-mers under the k-mer count cutoff

metagraph_results$V32 <- NA #Insertion of an additional column, which needs to be empty in your TSV results file 
#Additionally, check for misplaced results in the metagraph results, can happen due to tabulation mistakes

rowlength = length(row.names(metagraph_results)) #Number of genes analyzed

for(rower in 1:rowlength) { #To go through all genes and their results, line by line 
  m = 2 #Important for output formatting, starts at 2 because the first cell of results should contain the gene name
  n = 2 
  t = 0 #To stop the while loop
  column = 3 #Column of the metagraph results data frame, starts at 3 because col 1 is a number and col 2 is the gene name => Only collects the results per fastq
  
  while(t != 1) { #While we are not at the end of the current line in the metagraph results data frame
    
    if(grepl('fastq', metagraph_results[rower,column]) == TRUE) { #1st If => if the cell contains a results => Usually fast file name + kmer counts
      z <- metagraph_results[rower, column] #Collects, line per line (rower), each result, using column which starts at 3
      column = column + 1 #On to the next column
      parts <- strsplit(z, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] #Separates all k-mer results
      l <- list() #Empty list for k-mer collection 
      l[[1]] <- parts[[1]] #Names the list by the name of the fastq file
      
      for(i in 2:length(parts)) { #Starts at 2 because 1 contains the name of the list => fastq file name
        
        if (grepl('-' , parts[[i]]) == TRUE) { #Second if, to check if the count is associated with a single k-mer, or if it's many k-mers sharing a similar count (1-7=16 for example)
          z = parts[[i]] #Takes the current k-mer count which was found to contain "-"
          kcount = strsplit(z, "=")[[1]] #Splits the count and the k-mer number
          klist = strsplit(kcount[[1]], "-")[[1]] #Splits the first and last k-mer sharing the similar count
          count = kcount[[2]] #Stores the count 
          
          for (k in as.numeric(klist[[1]]):as.numeric(klist[[2]])) { #From the first k-mer to the last k-mer
            l[[k+2]] <- count } } #Do not forget that the k-mers starts at 0, that position 1 is full (file name) so +2, for each k-mer number => Makes seeing missing k-mers easier, as their                                    #positions are null in the list l 
        
        else{  #Else of the second if, means single k-mer with its count
          k = parts[[i]] 
          kcount = strsplit(k, "=")[[1]]
          knum = as.numeric(kcount[[1]])
          count = kcount[[2]] # Same processing, without needing the "-" separation step, and no loop
          l[[knum+2]]=count } }
      
      absent = 0 #Counter for the absent k-mers
      lower = 0 #Counter for the k-mers under the cutoff
      
      for(i in 2:length(l)) { #To go through each k-mer, including empty positions in the list (missing k-mers)
        
        if (is.null(l[[i]]) == TRUE) { #Tests for absent k-mers
          absent = absent + 1 }
        
        else if (as.numeric(l[[i]]) < k_cutoff) { #If not absent, then we check for the cutoff => The lower count is only for present k-mers
          lower = lower + 1 } }
      
      totalkmer = (length(l) - 1) #Total number of k-mers 
      coverage =  (totalkmer - absent)/totalkmer #Coverage 
      
      results_full[rower,1] = metagraph_results[rower,"V2"] #for each results we add the gene name
      results_cutoff[rower,1] = metagraph_results[rower,"V2"]
      
      results_full[rower,m]<-l[[1]] #The file name
      results_full[rower,m + 1]<- coverage #The coverage
      results_full[rower,m + 2]<- lower #And the number of k-mers under the cutoff
      
      if (coverage > coverage_cutoff) {
        if(lower < lower_cutoff) {
           results_cutoff[rower,n]<-l[[1]] #The file name
           results_cutoff[rower,n + 1]<- coverage #The coverage
           results_cutoff[rower,n + 2]<- lower 
           n = n + 3 } } #And the number of k-mers under the cutoff 
      m = m + 3 } #3 by three 
    
    else{ #Else of 1st if, means that we finished analyzing the current because no fastq was found using grepl
      t = 1 } } }

#Optional library(writexl)
#Optional write_xlsx(results_full, "results.xlsx") #Write out the output in the working directory
```
